statements.text: If your best language flex is macros, the language is probably doing too much.
Pattern matching is mostly just prettier if-else with a better PR team.
Operator overloading is a party trick that ages badly in real codebases.
Haskell-style type magic impresses devs more than it helps teams ship.
If your language needs monads explained at the bar, it already lost.
Dependent types are brilliant in theory and a productivity tax in practice.
Metaprogramming is often just writing bugs that compile before runtime.
The cooler the language feature sounds, the less often it earns its keep.

context.audience: software developers having a beer and some snacks
context.additional_context: Many developers brag about very specific language features but are those features worth bragging about
context.additional_instructions: come up with examples and let's see if the developers in the bar agree with it
